title: Sync Ascora Jobs into RestoreAssist — Training Data & Reporting in Minutes
description: Learn how to connect your Ascora account to RestoreAssist and bulk-import historical jobs for training benchmarks, pricing analysis, and IICRC compliance reporting.
transcript: The Ascora integration in RestoreAssist lets you sync your historical job history into the platform for training benchmarks, pricing analysis, and IICRC compliance reporting. Connect your API key, set a minimum job value filter to focus on private or larger-loss jobs, and optionally apply a CPI price uplift factor to adjust historical rates to current market pricing. The sync runs in dry-run mode first so you can preview what will be imported before committing to the database.
videoScript:
G'day — in this video I'm going to show you how to connect your Ascora account to RestoreAssist and sync your historical job data. This is one of the most powerful onboarding features we have, because it means you can start generating training benchmarks and pricing analysis from your existing job history on day one — you don't have to re-enter anything manually.

First, a quick overview of why you'd want to do this. If you've been running jobs in Ascora for any period of time, you have a wealth of data: job types, affected areas, equipment used, labour hours, and invoice values. RestoreAssist turns that data into actionable insights — pricing benchmarks against current market rates, IICRC compliance gaps in historical scopes, and training material for technicians based on real jobs your company has completed.

To get started, go to Settings, then Integrations, and click Connect next to Ascora. You'll need your Ascora API key, which you can find in your Ascora account under System Settings. Enter the key, and RestoreAssist will verify connectivity immediately.

Once connected, you can trigger a sync from the Integrations page or via the API. There are a few parameters worth understanding. The minimum job value filter — set this to 1000 dollars or higher if you want to focus on private or larger-loss jobs and exclude small make-safes or emergency callouts. This keeps your benchmark data meaningful.

The price uplift factor is particularly useful if your Ascora history goes back several years. Australian restoration pricing has increased significantly — rough CPI uplift for the sector is around 12% since 2023. If you set an uplift factor of 1.12, RestoreAssist adjusts all historical invoice values to 2025 dollar terms before running any pricing analysis. This means your benchmarks reflect what you'd charge today, not what you charged in 2022.

The first time you run a sync, I'd strongly recommend using dry-run mode. This previews exactly which jobs will be imported, how they'll be categorised, and what the uplifted pricing looks like — without writing anything to your database. Review the output, check that the job categorisation looks right, and then run the sync properly.

Once the sync completes, your historical Ascora jobs appear in the Inspections list tagged as imported. You can open any of them, review the scope, and run it through the IICRC compliance check to see how your historical documentation holds up against current standards. This is excellent material for internal training — it shows technicians the gap between what was documented in the past and what current best practice requires.

For ongoing jobs, the integration also supports inbound job dispatch via webhook. When a new job is dispatched in Ascora, it can automatically create a new Inspection in RestoreAssist — so your team never has to manually enter a job header again.

If you're an Ascora user and you want to get more value from your existing job history, the integration is available on the Professional plan. Head to restoreassist.app to start your free trial, and if you have questions about the Ascora connection, there's a setup guide in the Help Centre. I'll see you in the next video.
